title: Problem 1.2 (Problem 4 in Dvořák–Ossona de Mendez–Wu)
statement_text: Determine the function $g:\mathbb{N}\rightarrow\mathbb{R}_{+}$, where $g(n)$ is defined as the maximum of the ratio $\frac{\chi_{f}(G)}{\rho(G)}$ over all $n$-vertex graphs $G$.
context_text: Dvořák et al. showed that $\chi_f(G)$ cannot be bounded by any function of $\rho(G)$, which naturally motivates quantifying how quickly the ratio $\chi_f(G)/\rho(G)$ can grow with the number of vertices. They established $\Omega(\log\log n)\leq g(n)\leq O(\log n)$, leaving an exponential gap.
attributed_to: Dvořák, Ossona de Mendez, and Wu
paper_contribution: Proves that $g(n)=(\log n)^{1-o(1)}$, almost fully resolving the problem by showing the truth is close to the $O(\log n)$ upper bound.
paper_title: Fractional chromatic number vs. Hall ratio

title: Problem 1.4 (Problem 5 in Dvořák–Ossona de Mendez–Wu)
statement_text: Do there for some constant $c>0$ exist graphs $G$ of arbitrarily large fractional chromatic number such that $\rho(G)\leq c$ and $\alpha_{\mathrm{deg}_{H}}(H)\geq|E(H)|/c$ for every $H\subseteq G$?
context_text: Motivated by the fact that restricting weight functions in the formula for $\chi_f(G)$ to $\{0,1\}$-functions can be too restrictive, Dvořák et al. proposed studying weight functions proportional to degrees of a subgraph as a natural alternative class, asking whether degree-weighted Hall-type ratios can still be bounded while $\chi_f$ is large.
attributed_to: Dvořák, Ossona de Mendez, and Wu
paper_contribution: Affirmatively solves the problem by constructing graphs with bounded Hall ratio, arbitrarily large fractional chromatic number, and $\alpha_{\mathrm{deg}_H}(H)\geq |E(H)|/c$ for all subgraphs $H$ (Theorem 1.5).
paper_title: Fractional chromatic number vs. Hall ratio
